title: ampelocissus leonensis
intro: Ampelocissus leonensis is a species of plant in the grape family native to Africa, specifically in the savannas of West and Central Africa. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ampelocissus leonensis include its use in treating various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Herbal preparations of Ampelocissus leonensis typically involve drying the leaves and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
